notes: MiniMax M2.7 AutoRound W4A16 on 4x Intel Arc Pro B70 via vLLM/XPU TP4. Strict quality gate passed before benchmarking: raw145 exact token hashes at n64/n256, semantic suite, 16-run arithmetic repeat, and extended sixpack. This run uses exact MiniMax router logits into llm-scaler ESIMD work-sharing INT4 MoE decode, default XPU FlashAttention v2, PIECEWISE XPU graph, block-size 256, max_num_batched_tokens 512, prefix cache disabled, async engine, greedy temperature 0. Increment over the alias-correct tiny-FP32 in-place allreduce result: VLLM_MINIMAX_QK_RMS_XPU_HELPER=1 with helper max tokens 4; the tiny FP32 allreduce path remains alias-correct via VLLM_XPU_CUSTOM_ALLREDUCE_TINY_FP32_INPLACE_MAX_NUMEL=2. Four p512/n1536 repeats: 88.794746, 88.771642, 87.411381, and 88.274652 output tok/s; mean 88.313105 output tok/s and 117.750807 total tok/s. vLLM benchmark JSON reports total tok/s for prompt+decode but does not expose a separate prefill tok/s metric here. No speculative decoding, no expert dropping, no router approximation, no quality-reducing quant change, no power-limit change. Caveat: two quality logs printed Bad address (src/pipe.cpp:367) during shutdown after successful completion and JSON write; recorded as shutdown noise to monitor, not a quality failure.
